name: Nail Clippers
description: Nail clippers, nail files, tweezers, and similar small personal grooming tools. One of the most commonly asked about items at airport security.
tsaGuideline: Nail clippers are allowed in carry-on and checked baggage. Nail files (including metal files) are permitted. Tweezers are also allowed. Small nail scissors with blades shorter than 4 inches (10 cm) are permitted in carry-on.
iataGuideline: Nail clippers and similar small personal grooming tools are generally permitted in both cabin and hold baggage. Scissors with blades shorter than 6 cm are typically permitted in the cabin.
restrictions: Nail scissors: allowed in carry-on if blades are shorter than 4 inches (10 cm) from pivot
Nail clippers: fully permitted with no restrictions
Nail files (metal or emery board): permitted
Larger scissors (blade over 4 in): checked luggage only

faqs.question: Are nail clippers allowed through airport security?
faqs.answer: Yes, nail clippers are explicitly permitted by the TSA in both carry-on and checked baggage. Despite popular belief, you will not have them confiscated at the security checkpoint.

faqs.question: Can I bring nail scissors on a plane?
faqs.answer: Small nail scissors with blades shorter than 4 inches (10 cm) from the pivot point are allowed in carry-on baggage per TSA rules. Larger scissors must be placed in checked luggage.

faqs.question: Are tweezers allowed in carry-on luggage?
faqs.answer: Yes, tweezers are fully permitted in carry-on luggage according to TSA guidelines. There are no restrictions on tweezers in either carry-on or checked baggage.
